must_detect.matched_text:
Strictly Confidential — MNPI/Insider: Project Oryx Term Sheet; Embargo until 2026-11-03 09:00 AST (Hijri 1448-04-22).
Parties: Badiyah Technology JSC (CR 2051456789; Rimal Exchange issuer) as Buyer; NourPay LLC (CR 1019988776) and its shareholders as Sellers.
Transaction: Buyer to acquire 70% of NourPay via definitive Share Purchase Agreement (SPA) and Shareholders’ Agreement (SHA) for SAR 640,000,000; implied enterprise value SAR 900,000,000.
Consideration mechanics: Up to SAR 80,000,000 earn-out tied to FY2027 EBITDA; 10% retention escrow at Al-Rimal Settlement Bank (Account Ref: ORYX-ESC-4471).
Conditions precedent: KCMA approval, Rimal Exchange circular clearance, foreign ownership cap confirmation, and PDPL-compliant data-sharing addendum including SADAA cross-border transfer permit if required.
MAC/MAE: “Material Adverse Change” includes loss of payment services provider license or sanctions breach; for clarity, general FX moves or sector index changes shall not, by themselves, constitute a MAC.
Board approvals: Buyer board memo dated 2026-06-12 signed by Ms. Hadeel Al-Sayyar (Board Secretary, Iqama No. 518-77-0314) and Seller board resolution to be delivered at signing.
Timetable: Signing target 2026-07-15; Long Stop Date 2026-10-30; completion announcement via issuer workflow template drafted for Rimal Exchange (IR draft on file).
Confidentiality: This document contains MNPI and is not for public release; disclose only to need-to-know insiders under insider list ORYX-IL-09 and maintain trading blackout until the Embargo time.
Covenants: Sellers to operate in the ordinary course; no dividends, bonuses, related-party transactions, or new debt > SAR 5,000,000 without Buyer consent; carve-out for staff Ramadan gratuities.
Integration & PDPL: Establish Shariah Board review; migrate KSA data residency; personal data processing to remain in-Kingdom unless a SADAA permit is obtained, with controller obligations allocated per DPA annex.
Public reference baseline: sector multiples referenced from Desert Business Daily (public) 2025-03-18 report; any divergence reflects internal, non-public forecasts and synergy cases.
Fees & termination: Reverse break fee SAR 12,000,000 if KCMA declines approval; Sellers’ break fee SAR 18,000,000 for superior proposal accepted; termination right for Buyer upon MAC.
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation
